LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY ORDER OF SALE OF DWELLING SHOULD NOT BE MADE. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY DEFICIENT. FIRST, MR. WILKINS DID NOT TIMELY FILE PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATING "[PERSONAL SERVICE] OF EACH DOCUMENT LISTED IN [CCP 704.770(B)(1)] ON AN OCCUPANT OF THE DWELLING OR, IF THERE IS NO OCCUPANT PRESENT AT THE TIME SERVICE IS ATTEMPTED, POST[ING] A COPY OF EACH DOCUMENT IN A CONSPICUOUS PLACE AT THE DWELLING" PURSUANT TO CCP 704.770(B)(2). (CAL. RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1300(C).) MR. WILKINS HAS DEMONSTRATED COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 704.770(B)(1) ONLY, AND NOT CCP § 704.770(B)(2). (SEE PROOF OF SERVICE, FILED JAN. 9, 2014.) SECOND, IN HIS APPLICATION, MR. WILKINS HAS NOT AVERRED UNDER OATH THE AMOUNT OF ANY LIENS OR ENCUMBRANCES ON THE DWELLING AND THE NAME AND ADDRESS OF EACH PERSON HAVE A LIEN OR ENCUMBRANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: DEBORAH NEVILLE 	703.
ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF WILKINS, JOHN

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SALE OF DWELLING IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SATISFY THE MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE TO STATE THE AMOUNT OF EACH LIEN AND ENCUMBRANCE AND THE ADDRESS OF EACH PERSON RECORDING THE LIEN AND ENCUMBRANCE. MOREOVER, BECAUSE DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO A HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION OF $175, 000, THE FAIR MARKET VALUE OF THE PROPERTY OF $284,000 AS SET FORTH IN THE APPRAISAL AND THE KNOWN LIENS (EXCLUDING THE ONE RECORDED BY PLAINTIFF) TOTAL $74,827.07, THE COURT CANNOT MAKE A DETERMINATION THAT THE SALE OF THE PROPERTY WOULD RESULT IN ANY PAYMENT TO PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARY BAYTOP'S MOTION TO VACATE VOID JUDGMENT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. "IF THE COMPLAINT IN THE DEFAULT ACTION IS SUFFICIENT TO APPRISE THE [DEFENDANT] OF THE NATURE OF THE [PLAINTIFF'S] DEMAND, IT IS IMMATERIAL THAT IT MIGHT HAVE BEEN SUBJECT TO A DEMURRER FOR FAILURE TO MAKE AN ALLEGATION NECESSARY TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION'" (MOLEN V. FRIEDMAN (1998) 64 CAL.APP.4TH 1149, 1157.) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

Order To Show Cause Why Order Of Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Made
Off Calendar Mar-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Why Order Of Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Be Made is denied, without prejudice. The motion is procedurally deficient. First, Mr. Wilkins did not timely file proof of service demonstrating "[personal service] of each document listed in [CCP 704.770(b)(1)] on an occupant of the dwelling or, if there is no occupant present at the time service is attempted, post[ing] a copy of each document in a conspicuous place at the dwelling" pursuant to CCP 704.770(b)(2). (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) Mr. Wilkins has demonstrated compliance with CCP 704.770(b)(1) only, and not CCP 704.770(b)(2). (See Proof of Service, filed Jan. 9, 2014.) Second, in his application, Mr. Wilkins has not averred under oath the amount of any liens or encumbrances on the dwelling and the name and address of each person have a lien or encumbrance. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Deborah Neville 703.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Void Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: H. Nelson Meeks, Esq., 415.989.9915, for plaintiff John Wilkins.
Nancy Conway, Esq., 415.241.1140, for defendant Mary Baytop.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT MARY BAYTOP'S Motion To Vacate Void Judgment. Denied. "If the complaint in the default action is sufficient to apprise the [defendant] of the nature of the [plaintiff's] demand, it is immaterial that it might have been subject to a demurrer for failure to make an allegation necessary to state a cause of action'" (Molen v. Friedman (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1149, 1157.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Order To Show Cause Re: Sale Of Dwelling
Off Calendar Mar-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JOHN WILKINS, PLAINTIFF APPEARING WITHOUT HIS ATTORNEY;
ATTY. NANCY M. CONWAY, TEL. NO. 241-1140 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE COURT EXPLAINED THE TENTATIVE RULING TO MR. WILKINS WHOSE ATTORNEY WOULD NOT BE APPEARING IN TODAY'S HEARING. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Re: Sale Of Dwelling is Denied without prejudice to being refiled. Plaintiff failed to satisfy the mandatory requirements of Code of Civil Procedure to state the amount of each lien and encumbrance and the address of each person recording the lien and encumbrance. Moreover, because defendant is entitled to a homestead exemption of $175, 000, the fair market value of the property of $284,000 as set forth in the appraisal and the known liens (excluding the one recorded by plaintiff) total $74,827.07, the court cannot make a determination that the sale of the property would result in any payment to plaintiff. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =302/hk

Default Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-28-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.
H. Nelson Meeks, Esq. appeared with plaintiff John Wilkins, who placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Mary Baytop.
Default entered against defendant on December 11, 2007.
Doe defendants dismissed on December 11, 2007.
Statement of Damages filed on December 11, 2007.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff John Wilkins, and against the Defaulted Defendant: Mary Baytop as follows:

Damages/Principal - $27,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - Amount yet to be determined.
Costs - $365.00.
Total Judgment - To Be Determined.
Counsel shall a proposed Judgment, and submit to the Court for review no later than Friday, March 21, 2008.
